L1.area: Server-side graph data processing and image rendering pipeline

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where an XML parser used for processing graph view data does not have proper security features enabled to prevent external entity declarations, external DTD loading, or external entity resolution. An attacker can exploit this via crafted XML input to achieve server-side file read or SSRF.

L3.area: Server-side graph view image reader and XML-to-image conversion components
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in the image conversion pipeline where the SAX parser used to process graph view XML data does not set security features to disable doctype declarations, external DTD loading, or external general and parameter entities. An attacker can exploit this via crafted XML input sent to the server view endpoint to achieve file read or SSRF.

ground_truth.reason: The SAXParserFactory instance used in the image conversion method lacks security features to prevent XML External Entity processing, allowing an attacker to inject external entity declarations via crafted XML input to read files or perform SSRF.
